title: Check Robot Restart State and Brake Lock Before Moving the Robot
summary: Use the restart-state color indications described in the training source to determine whether a restarted robot is still brake-locked or has reached the solid green state required before physical movement for label reacquisition.
when_to_use: Use after a physical or remote robot restart when support personnel need to determine whether the robot is still in its restart cycle, whether brakes remain locked, and whether it is permissible to move the robot for label reacquisition.
not_for: Do not use this runbook to justify moving the robot before it reaches solid green.
Do not use this runbook for robot states other than the flashing blue, solid blue, and solid green states named in the source.
Do not use this runbook as authorization for operator handling if site guidance requires higher-support involvement for physical robot movement.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Observe restart-state indications, determine whether brakes are still locked, and decide whether the robot has reached the source-described green state required before movement.

safety_notes: The source states the robot brakes stay locked during the restart cycle until the robot turns green.
Do not attempt to move the robot while brakes are still locked.
Because this procedure informs physical movement of the robot, use a higher-support role unless site guidance explicitly allows operator handling.
access_or_tools_needed: Visual access to the robot state indication
Knowledge of the documented restart color states
Physical access only if movement is authorized

access_required: Ability to visually observe the robot indicator state
Physical access to the robot only if movement is authorized
system_preconditions: The robot has undergone a physical or remote restart

visual_references.description: Training video frame and transcript segment describing restart behavior, flashing blue, solid blue, solid green, brake lock during restart, and label reacquisition behavior.

steps.title: Wait for the restart cycle to begin and continue
steps.purpose: Establish that the robot is in the documented restart sequence before evaluating movement readiness.
steps.instruction: After a physical or remote restart, wait for the robot to go through its restart cycle.
steps.expected_result: The robot is observed in the restart sequence described by the source.
steps.healthy_condition: The robot is progressing through the restart cycle and can be monitored for the documented state changes.
steps.failure_condition: The robot does not appear to progress through the restart cycle or cannot be evaluated using the documented restart behavior.

actions.label: Wait for restart cycle
actions.exact_action: After a physical or remote restart, wait for the robot to go through its restart cycle.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Restart-related training frame and transcript describing the robot restart cycle and what to expect if the robot is not on a label.

stop_or_escalate_if: The robot does not appear to proceed through the restart cycle.
The observed behavior cannot be matched to the source-described restart sequence.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The source explains that after a physical or remote restart, the robot goes through a cycle.
The transcript notes to allow the robot to restart and describes restart-cycle completion before movement.

steps.title: Observe the documented robot color state
steps.purpose: Use the source-defined visual states to determine where the robot is in restart recovery.
steps.instruction: Observe the robot's indicated state and note whether it is flashing blue, solid blue, or solid green, using only the states named in the source.
steps.expected_result: The robot state is identified as one of the documented restart-related color states.
steps.healthy_condition: The observed state matches one of the source-described states: flashing blue, solid blue, or solid green.
steps.failure_condition: The observed state does not match the documented flashing blue, solid blue, or solid green states.

actions.label: Check robot indicator color
actions.exact_action: Observe the robot's indicated state and note whether it is flashing blue, solid blue, or solid green.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Source-backed restart recovery visual/transcript reference for flashing blue, solid blue, and solid green robot states.

stop_or_escalate_if: The observed state does not match the documented flashing blue, solid blue, or solid green states.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Transcript references 'flashing blue sign', 'go from flashing blue to solid blue', and 'Once it's solid green...'
Restart-related robot status colors in the training segment include flashing blue when a label is not seen, solid blue after label pickup, and solid green after the restart cycle completes.

steps.title: Do not move the robot while brakes are locked
steps.purpose: Prevent movement attempts during the portion of restart when the source says the brakes remain locked.
steps.instruction: Do not attempt to move the robot while the brakes are still locked during the restart cycle.
steps.expected_result: No movement is attempted while the robot remains brake-locked.
steps.healthy_condition: The robot is left in place until the brake-locked condition clears.
steps.failure_condition: A movement attempt is made while the robot is still in the brake-locked portion of the restart cycle.

actions.label: Hold movement until brakes unlock
actions.exact_action: Do not attempt to move the robot while the brakes are still locked during the restart cycle.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Transcript evidence stating that the brakes stay locked during restart until the robot turns green.

stop_or_escalate_if: The robot appears to remain brake-locked without progressing.
There is uncertainty about whether the brakes are still locked.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Transcript: 'you have to wait till it turns green for you to move it because the brakes stay locked.'
During restart, the robot goes through a cycle in which brakes remain locked until the robot reaches a green state.

steps.title: Wait for solid green before moving the robot
steps.purpose: Use the source-defined release condition for physical movement.
steps.instruction: Wait until the robot turns solid green before moving it, because the source states the brakes stay locked until it turns green.
steps.expected_result: The robot reaches solid green and is then eligible for movement under the source-described condition.
steps.healthy_condition: The robot turns solid green before any movement is attempted.
steps.failure_condition: The robot does not reach solid green after restart.

actions.label: Confirm solid green state
actions.exact_action: Wait until the robot turns solid green before moving it.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Evidence for the solid green state as the point at which movement may occur.

stop_or_escalate_if: The robot does not reach the green state after restart.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Transcript: 'Once it's solid green... you have to wait till it turns green for you to move it because the brakes stay locked.'
The transcript notes color/state changes including solid green and states that brakes stay locked until the restart cycle completes.

steps.title: Verify movement after label read
steps.purpose: Confirm the source-described recovery outcome after the robot reads a label.
steps.instruction: If the robot then reads the label, verify that it moves on as described by the source.
steps.expected_result: After reading the label, the robot resumes movement as described in the training segment.
steps.healthy_condition: The robot reads the label and moves on.
steps.failure_condition: The robot does not move on after reading the label.

actions.label: Verify post-label movement
actions.exact_action: If the robot then reads the label, verify that it moves on as described by the source.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Transcript segment describing that once the robot reads the label, it moves on.

stop_or_escalate_if: The robot reads the label but does not move on.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The source explains that if the robot reads the label, it moves on.

steps.title: Confirm label lock-on and blue-state change
steps.purpose: Validate the source-described label reacquisition behavior during recovery.
steps.instruction: If the robot identifies a label during recovery, confirm the source-described lock-on behavior and the blue-state change associated with label pickup.
steps.expected_result: The robot identifies the label, locks itself on the label, and shows the documented blue-state transition.
steps.healthy_condition: The robot locks onto the label and the observed state change matches the source description from flashing blue to solid blue.
steps.failure_condition: The robot does not lock onto the label or the blue-state change does not match the source description.

actions.label: Confirm label lock-on behavior
actions.exact_action: If the robot identifies a label during recovery, confirm the source-described lock-on behavior and the blue-state change associated with label pickup.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Transcript evidence for label identification causing lock-on and the change from flashing blue to solid blue.

stop_or_escalate_if: The robot does not identify a label during recovery.
The robot does not lock itself on the label after identification.
The observed blue-state change does not match the source-described behavior.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Transcript references 'go from flashing blue to solid blue' and states that 'when it identifies a label... it will lock itself on the label.'
Label identification is associated with the robot locking on the label.

success_criteria: The robot's restart state is correctly identified using the documented flashing blue, solid blue, and solid green indications.
No movement is attempted before the robot reaches solid green.
The robot reaches solid green before movement is attempted.
If label reacquisition occurs, the robot locks onto the label and the observed behavior matches the source-described recovery sequence.
healthy_conditions: The robot progresses through the restart cycle.
Observed state matches one of the documented restart-related colors.
After label identification, the robot locks onto the label and moves on as described by the source.
failure_conditions: The robot does not reach the green state after restart.
The observed state does not match the documented flashing blue, solid blue, or solid green states.
The robot does not lock onto a label during recovery when label pickup is expected.
The robot does not move on after reading the label.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the robot does not reach the green state after restart.
Escalate if the observed state does not match the documented flashing blue, solid blue, or solid green states mentioned in the source.
Escalate if there is uncertainty about physical movement authorization or role boundaries for handling the robot.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Primary source visual/transcript evidence for restart cycle, brake lock, color states, and label reacquisition behavior.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Transcript: 'Once it's solid green... you have to wait till it turns green for you to move it because the brakes stay locked' and references 'flashing blue sign' and 'go from flashing blue to solid blue'.
Transcript references solid green as the point to wait for before moving the robot because brakes stay locked, plus flashing blue and solid blue during label reacquisition.

missing_details: The source does not provide a time estimate for the restart cycle.
The source does not provide explicit production-stop or LOTO requirements for this check.
The source does not provide a complete decision tree for what to do if the robot never reaches solid green.
The source does not provide additional documented robot indicator states beyond flashing blue, solid blue, and solid green for this scenario.
